task_name: extract service layer from route handlers

plan_metrics.plan_content:
Here's the complete plan I've designed after thoroughly reading every file:

---

## What I Found

The codebase is a TypeScript/Express.js API with two route files and an in-memory data store. There is **no existing service layer** — all business logic lives directly in the route handlers. The tests are integration tests (supertest) that test the full HTTP stack, so they'll continue to pass as long as the HTTP surface (status codes, response shapes) is preserved.

---

## The Plan (7 Steps)

### New files to create
- **`src/errors.ts`** — A single `ServiceError extends Error` class with a `statusCode: number` property. Route handlers catch this and map it directly to `res.status(err.statusCode).json({ error: err.message })`.
- **`src/services/product.service.ts`** — `listProducts`, `getProductById`, `adjustProductStock`
- **`src/services/order.service.ts`** — `listOrders`, `getOrderById`, `createOrder`, `updateOrderStatus`

### Files to modify
- **`src/types.ts`** — Append new view-model interfaces (`ProductFilters`, `ProductListResult`, `ProductDetail`, `OrderFilters`, `OrderListResult`, `EnrichedOrder`, `EnrichedOrderItem`). Existing interfaces are untouched.
- **`src/routes/products.ts`** — Strip out all business logic; replace with service calls + try/catch
- **`src/routes/orders.ts`** — Same treatment

### Files left untouched
`src/app.ts`, `src/data.ts`, all test files

---

## The Boundary Rule

| Stays in route handlers | Moves to services |
|---|---|
| `parseInt`/`parseFloat` on params/query | Filtering & aggregation logic |
| `typeof x !== "number"` body type checks | `isLowStock`, `inventoryValue` computation |
| Structural body validation (`!items`, `!Array.isArray`) | Product/order lookup with 404 errors |
| `res.json()` / `res.status().json()` | Stock mutation & validation |
| `try/catch(ServiceError)` mapping | State machine (status transitions) |
| | Order total calculation & rounding |
